transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Good afternoon. Final weekly call for Sprint 4. I want a complete picture before we go into the weekend. Priya — set the stage.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Sprint 4 Week 2 summary — the headline is that we are in a significantly better position than we were last Friday. All 34 test cases are now passing on prod. The async queue is built and deployed. Redis is stable. UAT is scheduled for Monday April 20. Client has been briefed. Retrospective is locked in for April 24.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: All 34 passing — Neha, confirm that for me.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: Confirmed sir. I ran the full suite this morning on prod — all 34 passing. Including the 2 that were intermittently failing since Week 1. Both now passing consistently. I ran each of those 5 times just to be sure. 5 out of 5 both times. Async queue fixed them completely.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: The async queue being done is honestly the biggest win of Week 2. Suresh, walk through what was delivered.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Async queue is fully operational. It handles three scenarios — payment failure with automatic retry, gateway-down fallback with DB persistence, and idempotency check to prevent duplicate transactions. Redis came up Wednesday 2pm. I had the queue complete by 9pm, deployed to prod at 10:30pm. Thursday morning I ran manual end-to-end tests across all three flows — all working correctly.

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Redis has been running 40+ hours now with no issues. I've set up a queue-depth alert — if pending items exceed 100, I get an immediate notification. I'll be monitoring all weekend. The infrastructure is solid.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: On the frontend — yesterday's session with Neha surfaced 2 edge cases I wasn't handling. Browser back button during payment now shows a proper warning modal. Loading spinner on slow connections is fixed. Both tested on iOS and Android this morning. I also sent a UI walkthrough document to client — screen by screen guide so their UAT testers know what to expect.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Client received it?

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Yes sir. Client confirmed receipt at 11am today. Their lead tester replied and said it was very helpful. They're ready for Monday.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: I've also sent the UAT scenario document — 34 scenarios with expected outcomes, edge case descriptions, and instructions. Client has everything they need to run UAT independently.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: UAT Monday April 20 — what time, who's available?

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: UAT starts at 10am Monday. Client's full payments team — 4 testers plus their tech lead. From our side, Neha and Rahul are on standby throughout the day. Suresh is available for any backend queries. I'll be in the session to coordinate.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: One thing I want everyone to be clear on — if anything comes up during UAT, we don't respond individually to the client. We take it to our internal group first, discuss, then respond together. No panic responses on client calls.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Agreed. Same protocol we discussed in the morning standup.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: Understood. I'm also available Monday if any UI question comes up.

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Infra monitoring is on all weekend. I'm watching Redis, app servers, and network. Any prod alarm and I respond within 10 minutes.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Good. And retrospective April 24 — Rahul, you're scheduling that?

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Yes sir. Calendar invite goes out today. Lessons learned from this sprint — Day 1 env delay, Day 3 demo failure, SSL cert issue, staging vs prod parity. We need to document all of it formally so Sprint 5 doesn't repeat the same patterns.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: I'll send the retro agenda template in advance so people can note their points before the session. Better use of everyone's time than trying to remember on the day.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Good. I want to say something before we close. Sprint 4 was genuinely hard. Environment issues on Day 1, the Wednesday demo failure, SSL cert discovery on Day 4 — multiple things went wrong through no fault of this team. But every single time, the team diagnosed the issue, communicated clearly, and fixed it. We're going into UAT with all 34 tests passing and a properly built async queue. That's a strong outcome given what this week looked like on Monday morning.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: Thank you sir. Though I'll be honest — I'm hoping Sprint 5 has a calmer first week.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Same. Especially if the infra dependencies are sorted in advance this time.

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: I'll be in Sprint 5 planning from Day 1 this time. Priya already confirmed that. We're not going to have another Monday morning environment surprise.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: That's the commitment. Alright everyone — good work this sprint. Clean UAT Monday. Retro the 24th. See you all on the other side.
